note: Generated /integrations/* pages. Slugs MUST NOT collide with hand-authored integration pages (cursor, claude-code, langchain, litellm, openai-sdk, anthropic-sdk, vercel-ai-sdk, openrouter, continue, zed, vscode, jetbrains, github-actions, gitlab-ci, docker-compose, cloudflare, env-vars, cursor-claude-code). HowTo steps drive the HowTo JSON-LD.

integrations.bluf: Point LlamaIndex's LLM at the kolm gateway base URL and every retrieval-augmented call is captured as an (input,output) pair, PII-redacted on the way in, and signed on the way out — so your RAG traffic becomes the training set for an owned specialist.
integrations.summary: LlamaIndex orchestrates retrieval and prompting; kolm sits underneath as the OpenAI-compatible gateway. Swap the base URL, keep your indexes and query engine, and start capturing the pairs that compile into a model you own.

setup_steps.name: Install kolm and sign up
setup_steps.text: Sign up at /signup for an API key. Install the kolm gateway with npm i -g github:kolm-ai/kolm.

setup_steps.name: Point LlamaIndex at the gateway
setup_steps.text: Set the OpenAI base URL in your LlamaIndex Settings.llm to the kolm gateway /v1 endpoint and use your kolm API key.

setup_steps.name: Run your queries
setup_steps.text: Run your normal LlamaIndex query engine. Each LLM call is captured into your tenant lake with PII redacted on the capture path.

setup_steps.name: Distill and compile
setup_steps.text: Once you have enough pairs, run kolm distill then kolm compile to produce a signed specialist model quantized to your hardware.

setup_steps.name: Verify the build
setup_steps.text: Run kolm verify on the artifact or replay the receipt at /verify-prod to confirm the signature chain and K-score.

integrations.proof_stat_context: A specialist distilled from captured traffic asks the right clarifying question 96.5% of the time in the trinity-500 benchmark.

faqs.q: Does kolm change my LlamaIndex indexes?
faqs.a: No. kolm only intercepts the LLM completion calls via the gateway base URL. Your vector store, retrievers, and query engine are unchanged.
PII is redacted on the capture path before anything is stored in your lake. The redaction runs inline in the gateway.

faqs.q: Can I keep using OpenAI underneath?
faqs.a: Yes. The gateway can route to OpenAI, Anthropic, or a local model while it captures pairs for distillation.

integrations.bluf: Configure Haystack's OpenAIGenerator with the kolm gateway base URL and your pipeline runs unchanged while kolm captures, redacts, and signs every generation — turning production RAG traffic into a compilable training set.
integrations.summary: Haystack pipelines stay exactly as they are; kolm is the OpenAI-compatible endpoint behind the generator node. Capture flows automatically into your lake and becomes the basis for an owned specialist.

setup_steps.name: Get a kolm key
setup_steps.text: Sign up at /signup and copy your API key.

setup_steps.name: Set the generator endpoint
setup_steps.text: In your Haystack OpenAIGenerator, set api_base_url to the kolm gateway /v1 endpoint and api_key to your kolm key.

setup_steps.name: Run the pipeline
setup_steps.text: Run your Haystack pipeline normally. Completions are captured into your tenant lake with inline PII redaction.

setup_steps.name: Compile a specialist
setup_steps.text: Run kolm distill and kolm compile to build a quantized model trained on the captured pairs.

setup_steps.name: Verify
setup_steps.text: Replay the signed receipt at /verify-prod or run kolm verify locally.

integrations.proof_stat_context: The resulting specialist can be quantized aggressively — a 32B model compresses to 17.9 GB INT4 in the verified matrix.

faqs.a: Any version whose OpenAIGenerator accepts a custom api_base_url works, since kolm speaks the OpenAI wire protocol.
The gateway adds a thin capture + redaction + receipt step. The published wrapper benchmark records the end-to-end overhead; the receipt is signed asynchronously.
Yes, the gateway is Apache-2.0 and self-hostable, so traffic and captures stay in your environment.

integrations.bluf: Point any n8n OpenAI or HTTP node at the kolm gateway and your automation's LLM steps are captured, redacted, and signed automatically — so a no-code workflow quietly builds the dataset for an owned model.
integrations.summary: n8n drives the workflow; kolm is the model endpoint behind your AI nodes. No custom code: change the credential base URL and capture begins.

setup_steps.name: Create a kolm key
setup_steps.text: Sign up at /signup and copy your API key.

setup_steps.name: Add an OpenAI credential in n8n
setup_steps.text: Create an OpenAI credential whose base URL points at the kolm gateway /v1 endpoint, using your kolm key.

setup_steps.name: Use it in your nodes
setup_steps.text: Reference that credential from your OpenAI / AI Agent nodes. Each call is captured into your lake with PII redaction.

setup_steps.name: Build a specialist
setup_steps.text: Run kolm distill then kolm compile on the captured pairs to produce a signed, quantized model.

setup_steps.name: Verify the receipt
setup_steps.text: Confirm the build at /verify-prod or with kolm verify.

integrations.proof_stat_context: An owned specialist serves automation steps at 1.24 s mean latency per the trinity-500 benchmark, with no per-token bill.

faqs.q: Do I need to write code?
faqs.a: No. You only change the base URL on an n8n OpenAI credential. The capture, redaction, and signing happen in the gateway.

faqs.q: Can I route to multiple providers?
faqs.a: Yes. The gateway speaks OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, vLLM, SGLang, TGI, and TRT-LLM protocols and can route between them.
In your tenant lake, which is self-hostable. PII is redacted before storage.

integrations.bluf: Configure DSPy's LM with the kolm gateway base URL and every optimized program call is captured and signed — so your compiled DSPy prompts feed straight into a distilled, owned specialist model.
integrations.summary: DSPy compiles prompt programs; kolm compiles the model. Wire DSPy's LM to the kolm gateway and the traces DSPy already produces become the training pairs for an owned model.

setup_steps.name: Sign up for kolm
setup_steps.text: Create an account at /signup and copy your API key.

setup_steps.name: Configure dspy.LM
setup_steps.text: Set dspy.LM to an OpenAI-compatible model with api_base pointed at the kolm gateway /v1 endpoint.

setup_steps.name: Run your DSPy program
setup_steps.text: Compile and run your DSPy module. Each LM call is captured with inline PII redaction.

setup_steps.name: Distill to a specialist
setup_steps.text: Run kolm distill and kolm compile to turn the captured pairs into a signed, quantized model.

setup_steps.name: Verify
setup_steps.text: Replay the receipt at /verify-prod.

integrations.proof_stat_context: Quantizing the distilled model is fast — 125.3 s for a 32B model on a single RTX 5090 in the verified matrix.

faqs.a: No. DSPy optimizes the prompt program; kolm compiles the underlying model. They compose: DSPy traces become kolm training pairs.
Any OpenAI-compatible backend, since the kolm gateway speaks the OpenAI protocol.
Yes. Every compile emits an Ed25519-signed receipt replayable at /verify-prod.
